title: Graze Me Away
tagline: Boutique catering specialist for events with grazing boxes and more
description:
Graze Me Away is a boutique catering business specialising in gourmet grazing boxes, platters, and styled grazing tables for all occasions. From intimate gatherings to large events, we craft visually stunning and delicious spreads using fresh, high-quality ingredients. Based in Coolaroo, Victoria, we pride ourselves on personalised service, unique presentation, and creating memorable experiences through food.
Products/Services:
Grazing Boxes
Perfect for gifts, picnics, or small gatherings. Available in various sizes and can be customised to include:
• Sweet, savoury, or mixed selections
• Breakfast or brunch options
• Seasonal fruits, cheeses, crackers, dips, deli meats, sweets, and more
• Dietary options available (GF, vegan, 100% halal)
Grazing Platters & Catering
Ideal for entertaining at home, parties, or work functions. Available in small to extra-large sizes with a wide range of inclusions:
• Cheese & charcuterie
• Fruit & dessert platters
• Brunch platters
• Kids platters
• Mini sliders, wraps, focaccias, and more
Styled Grazing Tables
For larger events like weddings, engagements, corporate events, baby showers, and birthdays. Each table is uniquely styled with:
• Rustic or modern setups
• Custom florals and props
• Variety of finger foods, fresh produce, pastries, sliders, and more
• Includes setup, styling, hire of stands and tableware.
Coolaroo VIC 3048
grazemeaway@gmail.com
0450 615 360

title: Shariah Collections
tagline: Online hijab, abaya and modest clothing
description:
At Shariah Collections, we are passionate about redefining modest fashion for Muslim women. Our journey began with a vision: to create clothing that reflects both elegance and practicality while staying true to the principles of modesty.
We understand the importance of comfort and functionality in everyday wear, which is why our designs feature lightweight, breathable, and wrinkle-free fabrics. Beyond comfort, we saw the need for a truly fuss-free khimar, one that is tie-back free and offers complete coverage without exposing any part of the hair or face. This commitment to solving real challenges in modest fashion inspired us to create designs that blend simplicity with sophistication.
Each piece in our collection is crafted with care, using only the finest fabrics and the utmost attention to detail. Our khimars and abayas are thoughtfully designed to prioritise modesty and comfort while delivering premium quality you can feel in every stitch
Broadmeadows VIC 3047

title: Zikkies Thrift Store
tagline: Thrift store located in the heart of Coburg
description:
Zikkies Thrift Store is more than just a secondhand shop—it’s a community hub where sustainable shopping meets social impact. We offer quality pre-loved clothing, homewares, and unique finds, with support from the National Zakat Foundation to uplift the Muslim community. Every purchase and donation contributes to a greater purpose, making secondhand shopping a choice that benefits both the community and the environment. Visit us in-store and be part of something meaningful!
Products:
– Clothing for men, women, and children
– Islamic wear for men, women, and children (including hijabs)
– Shoes and accessories
– Homewares and decor (including Islamic art and prayer mats)
– Books and educational resources, including Islamic books
– Toys and games
– Small furniture pieces
Services:
– Community-driven thrift shopping experience
– Donation drop-off point for preloved goods
– Affordable, sustainable fashion and homeware options
– Volunteering opportunities
– Community building initiatives
428 Sydney Rd, Coburg VIC 3058
melbourne@zikkies.org.au
(03) 7013 0521

title: Organic Instinct
tagline: Health and wellness store in Coolaroo run by qualified nutritionist
description:
At Organic Instinct, our aim is to ensure that we provide alternatives to all your basic health needs, without the chemical overload.
Our Organic Instinct journey began when we became parents for the first time. There was an overwhelming sense of responsibility to give our little person the best start in life. We began looking more closely at the products we had in our own home and the source of the foods we ate. One product and one food at a time, we changed or substituted them over to the low tox and organically grown varieties. We educated ourselves about ingredient lists, chemical lists, food additives, the benefits of supplements, aromatherapy and general health and wellness.

– Nikkah according to Quran & Sunnah
– Civil marriages according to the Marriage Act
Born in Melbourne, Australia, Baha Yehia was brought up by proud Lebanese migrants Khodr and Mona Yehia in a family of 11. He attended local primary and secondary schools in Brunswick where he became proficient in both the English and Arabic language.
Baha became an authorised celebrant in 2017 after completing a certificate IV in Celebrancy. He opted for the option of being a Religious Celebrant which allows him to conduct civil and religious ceremonies.

tagline: Educational institute teaching Islamic tradition in Western Sydney since 2004
description:
AlBayan Institute was established in 2003 by few brothers and Sheikh Tarek El-Bikai who has dedicated numerous years acquiring and developing his traditional Islamic sacred knowledge from some of the most respected and qualified scholars of our time.
AlBayan Institute also caters for older hearts with the introduction of the adult syllabus in 2009. This syllabus is taught by respected Islamic scholars and encompasses a wide variety of subjects preparing seekers of knowledge for their afterlife. title: Moroccan Soup Bar
tagline: Trendy Moroccan vegetarian cuisine at new North Melbourne location est. 1998
description:
A Melbourne institution in the heart of North Melbourne. The Moroccan Soup Bar brings together a unique approach to the taste sensations of Morocco with a diversity of cultural experiences, in an environment of generosity and abundance. We offer delivery takeaway pick up and pre order weekly meals. We also offer private functions for small groups. Our menu offers a range of vegetarian dishes including our famous Chickpea Bake.
Hana Assafiri is a much-loved and revered social activist and radical entrepreneur. Through the medium of food and dining in her renowned Moroccan Soup Bar, Hana has worked tirelessly to rectify the systemic and social barriers to women’s empowerment.
In 1998, she opened the Moroccan Soup Bar, which would quickly become an iconic Melbourne institution—founded on the radical notion that marginalised women, together in the kitchen, can effect social change.

title: Al Ihsan Foundation
tagline: Charity and international public relief organisation established in 2014
description:
In 2014, Al-Ihsan Foundation was established by compassionate and dedicated individuals, including Abu Ahmad and Ahmed Dannoun, along with other caring members of the wider Sydney community. A spark of compassion ignited within our hearts, prompted by the challenges posed by the Somalia famine and the devastating Syrian war at that time.
As witnesses to the heart-wrenching challenges of the Somalia famine and the harrowing Syrian war, we couldn’t remain mere spectators; we felt compelled to become part of the solution. Driven by an unwavering commitment to make an impactful difference, we recognised the urgent need for action, compelling us to initiate lasting change. Al-Ihsan Foundation was born from our shared belief that we can be change-makers for lasting transformation.
We chose the name Ihsan, meaning `perfection` or `excellence. Our aspiration is to lead in providing aid and assistance to the most vulnerable and often forgotten members of our society. We hold firmly to our principles of carrying out our work with Ihsan, compassion, empathy, and dignity.
✓
Registered Charity
✓
Tax Deductible

title: Holland Park Mosque
tagline: Longest continually opened mosque opened in 1908 situated in Holland Park
description:
The Holland Park Mosque is the oldest Masjid on the East Coast of Australia and has consistently been opened for over 100 years. Established in 1908 by Afghan cameleers, this 114 year old site is a pioneer for all Islamic societies, and a major spiritual hub of Brisbane and its surrounding cities. Boasting a rich history, the Holland Park Mosque is a proud testament to how Muslims have been a part of the Australian fabric for over a century. We serve the growing needs of the Muslim community, while providing a safe, peaceful and inclusive space to all members of the community. The Holland Park Mosque was featured in the highly rated “The Mosque Next Door” TV series by SBS. Please feel welcome at the Holland Park Mosque.Queensland (QLD)
Women prayer area on the first floor. Womens toilets and wudu area available on first floor as well.
309 Nursery Rd, Holland Park QLD 4121
hpmosque@gmail.com

title: Gatton Mosque
tagline: Mosque located in the town of Gatton en route to Toowomba from Brisbane
description:
Managed by the Lockyer Valley Islamic Association (LVIA) located in Gatton, Queensland (QLD)
The Lockyer Valley Islamic Association was established in 2011 in Gatton, Queensland to serve the needs of the local Muslim Community.
The Lockyer Valley is an hour west of Brisbane and 30 mins from Toowoomba.
The region includes Gatton, Laidley, Plainland and the surrounding towns and has a total population of around 41000.
Over the years the Muslim population in the Valley has been mainly migrants from Turkey, Iraq, Afghanistan and a sprinkling from other countries and more the recent migrants include the Sudanese, Burmese Rohingya and Bangladeshi Muslims as well as a student population from Malaysia, Indonesia and Pakistan who have made the Valley their home.
The Gatton Mosque is open for the 5 daily Salah (prayers), including Juma’ah (Friday) Prayers, and Taraweeh Salah in Ramadan.
225 Woodlands Road, Gatton QLD 4343
admin@lvia.org.au

title: Sofra Kebab House
tagline: Casual counter-service joint making rustic Middle Eastern specialties
description:
Home to Adelaide’s Legendary Kebabs since ’98!
A JOURNEY FROM 1998
In the heart of Adelaide, back in 1998, a dream blossomed. Our family, with a rich lineage of culinary expertise, aimed to craft a space where food wasn’t just consumed but celebrated. Sofra wasn’t merely birthed as a Kebab House; it was our homage to a legacy of flavors from the crossroads of Iraq and Turkey. Today, we proudly stand as one of Adelaide’s oldest kebab destinations, still nurturing the same dream, passion, and of course, the same family touch.
BEYOND KEBABS – A CULINARY PALETTE
Our menu, though rooted in traditional Iraqi-Turkish inspirations, boasts a vibrant array of offerings. From the savory Yiros that promise a burst of freshness with each bite, the legendary Kebabs that have left many speechless, to the eclectic mix of Salads and Dips that complement every dish, we ensure that Sofra remains a haven for every food lover.
Unit 3/60 West Terrace, Adelaide SA 5000
(08) 7001 6343

title: Masjid St Marys – IPDC Centre
tagline: Mosque located in Western Sydney home to the IPDC
address: 117-119 Forrester Road, North St Marys NSW 2760
description:
Corner of Glossop Street and Forrester Road. Entry via Glossop Steet.
(Immediate left turn after turning left at the lights from Forrester Road)
Masjid St Marys was first established in 2008 as a prayer facility for the locals in St Marys. However it now has grown as a hub for Muslims around the Western Sydney area with many scopes and services. The general scopes of Masjid St Marys include:
Fulfil the need for prayer facilities in the neighbourhood
Weekend Arabic and Quranic School for Children in the form of Al Tadhkirah Institute
Rich Library Facilities
Foodbank Services
Multi-Faith Dialogue Sessions
Migrant Support, Youth and Children’s Activities
Periodic Multicultural Community Gathering and Open Day
Periodic Learning Sessions conducted by Renowned Scholars
Community and Social Services
Daily Iftars and Khatmul Quran Taraweeh during Ramadan
These projects are initiatives of the
Islamic Practice and Dawah Circle
(IPDC)
The Masjid Expansion Project
Alhamdulillah, St Marys is evolving fast. With plans for the new Metro Station just 500 meters away and the new Western Sydney Airport nearby. We have also witnessed a significant growth in our Muslim community in St Marys requiring urgent expansion of our Masjid.
We are excited to announce that we plan to expand by purchasing land opposite our current location within the next six months. This new land is 70% larger than our current site and will cost $3.4 million.
117-119 Forrester Road, North St Marys NSW 2760
masjidstmarys@ipdc.org.au
0404 936 792

description:
The Islamic Centre, Islamic Society of Palmerston, Northern Territory (NT)
The Society is serving locals of Palmerston and surrounding rural Darwin region for more than a decade. The Society regularly invites local Muslims as well as other faith people to understand Islam in a peaceful multicultural environment.
Palmerston is second largest and fastest growing city of Northern Territory, Australia. The Society has secured 7,100 square meter land parcel in Palmerston to build an Islamic Center with few obligations. The Society currently has a temporary prayer hall (future IMAMA’s residence) where it runs daily and Friday prayers. However, to meet current and growing community needs and to preserve identity of local Muslims, the Society has developed plans to build the Islamic Centre.

Al-Taqwa College was originally named the Islamic School of Werribee. It was one of the very first Islamic educational institutions in Victoria and currently the largest one in operation. Some quick facts about our College:
* Located in Truganina, Victoria

* Operates Camp Coorong

title: Mt Hira College
tagline: Islamic Co-ed Prep to Year 12 school in South East of Melbourne established in 2000
description:
Islamic Co-ed Prep to Year 12 school in South East of Melbourne
From its humble origins of 20 founding students and a handful of portable classrooms, Mt Hira College is a testament to its truly ambitious and driven community. The College stands almost two decades strong, catering fully from prep to Year 12 and very proud of its phenomenal growth and multicultural diversity. Our team of dedicated teaching and non-teaching staff take pride in working collaboratively to inspire our students, guiding them to aim higher and enhance their learning capabilities every single day. Mt Hira College enables educational and spiritual development, a safe and caring environment for young minds to grow and explore.
Home to close to 1,500 students from Foundation to Year 12 with more than 135 caring staff we’re an educational community representing over 40 cultures and backgrounds. We are proud of our multicultural ethos and the way our community collaborates to make a difference. With a rigorous curriculum ICOM is built on the best of Victorian and Australian educational standards.

tagline: Mosque located on Albany Highway inside the Pioneer Village
description:
Armadale Masjid and Islamic Centre (AMIC) is a fully fledged Masjid and Islamic Centre inside the iconic Pioneer Village at Armadale along the Albany Hwy.
All the required facilities for a Masjid are available here. This is also a dawah hub and open to all Muslims and non-muslims. It has huge car parking, ladies prayer and functions amenities and kids sports facilities. The Mosque building is truly a magnificent structure, built in a turn of the century theme from heritage bricks with jarrah windows, staircases and mezzanine floor in 1970s. Islamic Practice and Dawah Circle Inc (IPDC) bought this property in 2016 and established as a Masjid and Islamic Centre.
Armadale Masjid is a centre of Islamic Practice and Dawah Circle Incorporated (IPDC).
Women’s prayer facilities are available.

title: Katanning Mosque
tagline: Remote mosque located in the community of Katanning
description:
The Katanning Mosque established in 1980, Western Australia (WA)
The Katanning Mosque was built in 1980 by the local Islamic community, who arrived in Katanning in 1974 after moving from Christmas Island, and was opened in 1981 by late Tunku Abdul Rahman, the first Prime Minister of Malaysia.
Katanning’s large Muslim community originated from the Cocos and Christmas Islands. The Cocos-Malays are descended from workers brought to the Cocos Islands in the 19th century to assist with the harvesting of coconuts for copra.

Aptly named after a ‘righteous woman in her own right’ Maryam (Mary) is presented as an example to all humankind. A once close-knit community religious centre that focussed on providing local Muslims a place of worship and Islamic teachings, values, and practices, it has since developed into more than just a prayer room. With the exponential growth within Wyndham municipality, which brings along multicultural residents, Virgin Mary Mosque sees the opportunity to fill the gap of being a community centre, education centre and religious centre.

title: Emir Sultan Mosque
tagline: Turkish inspired mosque in the heart of Dandenong
description:
ICMG Dandenong Emir Sultan Mosque was founded in 1991 as a place of worship for local Muslim families to educate their children with Islamic essentials. Originally the Mosque was purchased as a church and then adapted into a Mosque. However, over the years, the population of the Muslims in the area was increasing dramatically. To keep up with the growth and demand for our services, we initiated a reconstruction project in 2012 which included a new mosque and additional buildings such as a community youth centre, funeral services and a wudhu area.
Main Women’s prayer facilities are upstairs, however small area also downstairs for handicapped persons and those with very young children. Access is from the right side of the masjid along the fence.

title: Granny’s Gozleme
tagline: Authentic handmade gozleme located in Galleria in the CBD
description:
Menu / Order
At Granny’s Gozleme we carry on a family tradition of cooking fresh gozleme daily using quality ingredients. They are served at the just the right temperature to be enjoyed right away. All of our gozleme is prepared and cooked in store using local produce from Queen Victoria Market and surrounding suburbs in Melbourne. I have fond memories of waking up to the smell of my grandma’s gozleme in the morning. My favourite in the morning was the spinach and cheese gozleme.
In Turkiye, gozleme can be eaten for breakfast, lunch or dinner. Families usually make gozleme in large quantities which are then stored in the freezer at home. They can easily be reheated in a microwave or sandwich press in a couple of minutes and ready to eat.”
b08/385 Bourke St, Melbourne VIC 3000

title: Accelerated Allied Health
tagline: Mobile allied health professionals providing physio, exercise physiology and occupational therapy
description:
We are a team of passionate allied health professionals, featuring Physiotherapists, Exercise Physiologists & Occupational Therapists that work holistically to help you achieve your health and fitness goals. Our mission is to assist you in developing healthy habits over the long-term, so that you make better health choices and live an independent lifestyle!
We work with clients on the NDIS, Workcover and Private Health. We are an NDIS provider, coming to the homes, local parks or gyms of our clients to work on their weaknesses and further refine their strengths! From an athletic population standpoint, we specialise in helping younger children, adolescents and adults prepare themselves optimally in their sport and or support them every step of the journey in their rehabilitation after an injury.

title: United Muslim Migrants Association (UMMA) Centre
tagline: Mosque in the heart of the Eastern Suburbs of Melbourne with adjacent sports hall and womens area
description:
Mosque in the heart of the Eastern Suburbs of Melbourne with adjacent sports hall and large womens area
UMMA Centre which was established in 1985 and is home to a diverse Muslim community, who began settling in Melbourne since the early 1960’s and 1970’s. Over the years UMMA Centre has grown significantly and now serves as a vibrant community, reflecting its commitment to faith,  education, wellbeing, and inclusivity. UMMA symbolises the successful establishment and growth of a dynamic, multicultural Islamic community in Melbourne. It has a sporting facility and also delivers programs such as Islamic classes for adults and youth, seminars, and events aimed at harmony and solidarity with the community at large.

Masjid | Gym+Pool | Charity | Restaurant | Counselling | GP | Halal Matchmaking & More
The Islamic Information and Services Network of Australasia (IISNA) is a not-for-profit organisation, dedicated to sharing authentic Islamic information and providing services which aim to benefit Muslims and Non-Muslims in the Australasia region.
We are located in the heart of the Islamic community in Broadmeadows
We provide services to help and provide benefits to Muslims and Non-Muslims across Australasia. We have many services including an Islamic school, cafe, swimming pool and separate gym for men and women.
Our current activities are daily prayers, indoor sports, drug and alcohol referral and counselling, family reconciliation services, childcare and religious education classes.

title: Australian International Academy – Kellyville Campus
tagline: Islamic independent Primary & Secondary School operating in Kellyville
description:
The Academy was originally established in 1983 as King Khalid Islamic College and is Australia’s first Islamic school.
The school has six campuses that offer the Australian National Curriculum and the International Baccalaureate (IB). The academy sports a Primary Campus (providing grades Prep to Grade 5) in Coburg, and a Secondary Campus (Grade 6 to Year 12) in Merlynston, North Coburg. A new campus, based in Caroline Springs commenced operation at the beginning of 2014.
The Academy has expanded into New South Wales by purchasing a school in Strathfield South (Noor Al Houda Islamic College, originally Leigh College) and an additional campus in Kellyville.
The Academy also has a campus in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates which in 2013 became a fully accredited International Baccalaureate (IB).
2 Foxall Rd, North Kellyville NSW 2155

title: Minaret College, Doveton Campus
tagline: Islamic College founded in 1992 offering co-educational learning from Prep to Year 6 at m Doveton campus
description:
Founded in 1992 as Islamic College of Noble Park by Mr Mohamed Hassan, Minaret College’s vision has always been to give a holistic education to children, helping them achieve their full potential and to develop into future leaders and pioneers.
Starting with only 22 students and one class teacher, Minaret has now grown to cater for more than 2500 students. Minaret College is dear to many within the community; we have a rich history and identity and continue to be guided by our strong vision for the future.
Minaret College offers Pre-School to Year 12 education at Springvale and Officer campuses. Our Doveton Campus is dedicated to primary years. We continually invest in our students to ensure they attain a quality Islamic and secular education.
Minaret College prides itself on nurturing the leaders of tomorrow. Each and every student is empowered to embody our motto: Faith, Knowledge and Practice.
146 Kidds Rd, Doveton VIC 3177

tagline: Impacting the community through Islamic finance and investments since 1998
description:
ICFAL started in 1998 as an effort to support Muslims in Australia to live a Riba (interest) free life. This required providing Shariah-compliant finance for housing, vehicle, and community infrastructure. Energized by a group of highly motivated volunteers in its early days, ICFAL has grown to a leading Islamic Finance provider throughout Australia.
ICFAL is a 100% member-funded and owned organisation.
So there is no question on the source of the fund – it’s coming from the members of ICFAL.
We have grown to 4,000+ members and have been around in Australia for over 25+ years.

title: Australian Islamic Centre
tagline: Iconic contemporary designed mosque located in central Newport
description:
The Australian Islamic Centre (AIC) represents a unique Islamic environment that integrates Australian values with the beauty of Islam. We’ve undergone quite the transformation over the years. From being a community centre that mainly serviced the local Muslim community in Newport and its surrounds, to becoming a global architectural icon that has attracted tourists to our great state from all over the world.
AIC has grown from being a place of worship to being a full-fledged centre with services that cater to many of the community’s needs.
We offer wide range of services from our complex. In addition to the daily prayers, we have: advice and counselling; education for children and adults; services related to birth, marriage and death; and many more.

title: Melbourne Multicultural Centre / Golden Wattle Masjid
tagline: Mosque located in the outskirts of Western Melbourne in Tarneit
description:
Melbourne Multicultural Centre (MMC) located in Tarneit, Victoria
Melbourne Multicultural Centre (MMC) is a project of Islamic Practice and Dawah Circle (IPDC) in Victoria. IPDC Incorporated is a registered Australian body with Australian Securities and Investment Commission (ASIC). In addition to propagate the basic teachings of Islam, IPDC is involved in a number of community services including advancement of education, eradication of poverty, promotion of better health, improvement of human welfare, quality of life and thereby attain the pleasure of Allah.
MMC is a Masjid and Islamic Centre project of IPDC in Victoria, Australia. It’s located at 700 Davis Road at Tarneit, a western suburb of Melbourne metropolitan area. The Wyndham city council has issued us a permit to use the property as a place of worship. The size of the property is 10 Acrs (40,000sq meters), one of the largest (in terms of area) Masjid complexes in Australia, Alhamdulillah. Approved Masjid complex will be used for Islamic activities and services that will benefit whole Australian Community.
Separate prayer facilities for Sisters
Al Tadhkirah Institute: Hifzul program and Weekend Quran class for all ages.

title: Supreme Halal Foods
tagline: Meat wholesaler supplying halal natural casings and meat exports
description:
Supreme Halal Foods Pty. Ltd. was established in 1990, by Rashid Essop, a leader in the Australian Muslim community and prominent businessman. Responding to the need for halal meat and casings that could be trusted and was of a high quality, he ensured a commitment to halal, quality and reliability that has enabled Supreme Halal Foods to become a leading international producer of halal, natural casings and a reliable Australian exporter of halal meat and qurban.
Today, we operate the only Australian casings plant that deals strictly with halal. We clean and calibrate halal, natural casings that we supply Australian butchers and manufacturers and we export to agents and manufacturers in Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Japan Fiji and South Africa. Our casings are used to produce top quality small goods and are also used for natural sutures.
In addition, we export halal meat and offal to commercial agents and Islamic charities, in several countries. We have managed the qurban for various international charities from Australia, Indonesia, Turkey, Germany, the UK and Holland. As part of the logistics of the qurban we have dealt with recipient charities in Africa, Indonesia, India, Jordan, Palestine, Lebanon, Kosovo and the Middle East.
41 Redgum Drv, Dandenong South VIC 3175

title: Ummah Aid
tagline: Charity and international aid organisation focused on alleviating poverty worldwide
description:
*Not affiliated with Ummah Directory
Ummah Aid is a not-for-profit organisation established by volunteers who are driven by their faith and want to make a difference in alleviating poverty and suffering worldwide. We do this by focusing on specific areas of need that impact many communities in the developing world. These include building water wells, providing emergency food relief, delivering medical aid and sponsoring orphans.
We assist with the short-term and long-term needs of communities. This includes responding quickly with aid to disaster victims and creating long-term solutions for communities, such as water wells, to promote healthy souls and bodies.
All donations we receive go 100% to the projects selected by donors. Administrative costs are funded from within the organisation, so we maximise the impact of donations to help those who need it most.

title: MATW Project
tagline: Charity and international aid organisation established in 2016 by Br. Ali Banat
description:
Born and raised in Sydney, Australia, Ali Banat, was an ordinary guy.  Successful and hard-working, Ali became a self-made businessman by the age of 29 years old.
Like many others his age with his success, he lived a life of abundance and luxury, sharing the fruits of his hard work with family and friends. He enjoyed life to the fullest, making the most of what the world had to offer.
He loved what he had and the life that he led.
It all changed with a deadly diagnosis. One that would serve as a reminder that time on earth is limited. A gift.
When Ali received this diagnosis, he began to reflect on the life that he had led up until that very point and decided that he hadn’t invested enough in his Akhirah. He couldn’t present what he had – all the material things he’d accumulated, to Allah SWT.
Ali had a choice to make; knowing that his life was drastically cut short. He could have easily fallen into despair, as is common with most people diagnosed with cancer.
Ali chose to see this diagnosis as an opportunity to make a difference in this world and for his Akhirah.
So he sold everything he had, booked a flight to Togo and thus began the journey for Muslims Around The World Project in 2016.
Using his own wealth, Ali began by feeding and supporting orphans and widows in the most impoverished communities within Togo. He invested in agricultural tools and tractors so the community could farm, helping lift themselves out of poverty. He built water wells so they could have clean water, a mosque to pray in, and a school for the children to learn and play.
He began to build a legacy that today has expanded to more than 20 countries around the world.
100% Donation Policy
MATW Project stands out as a distinguished Muslim charity committed to supporting those in need within the Islamic community through Sadaqah and Zakat donations. Embracing a
100% donation policy
, we ensure that every penny contributed by donors directly benefits the intended causes, ranging from humanitarian aid to developmental projects. Our transparent and efficient approach in handling donations maximises the impact on the lives of recipients, making MATW Project a trustworthy platform for fulfilling one’s charitable Islamic duties.
As a Muslim charity, MATW Project plays a pivotal role in facilitating the practice of Sadaqah and Zakat, two fundamental pillars of Islamic giving. Our dedication to channeling the entirety of donations towards helping the less fortunate highlights our commitment to upholding Islamic values of compassion and generosity. By providing a seamless and secure donation process, MATW Project empowers individuals to make a meaningful difference in the world, aligning their contributions with the teachings of Islam and the spirit of giving that defines the Muslim community.
✓
Registered Charity
✓
Tax Deductible
Level 2/47 Rickard Rd, Bankstown NSW 2200

title: Uthman’s Dream
tagline: Charity and international aid organisation supporting communities in Northern Uganda
description:
At the tender age of 8, Uthman Sudais was orphaned after losing his parents due to the war and violence in Northern Uganda in 1993. Uthman understands first hand the disadvantages and traumas of an uncertain future and has dedicated his life to making a difference, rebuilding the impoverished communities in Uganda.
What is his
dream
? Growing up in North Uganda, Uthman vividly remembers traveling 20 km to and from his village barefoot on the scorching hot ground to collect water. Uthman carried out this trip multiple times a day and was paid 41 cents per day. Uthman endured this pain until he was able to save up for a pair of shoes to protect his feet from the hot sun costing $1.40. This is one of the many hardships that Uthman faced.
Uthman’s upbringing and life experiences has fuelled a lifelong
dream
to provide communities in need with food, water, shelter, education and employment opportunities.
UDI is a not-for-profit organisation currently operating with sponsors and donations with no administration costs involved. We believe transparency is key, with all donations being utilised sustainably and directly to those in need. Accountability reports provided upon request. As a grassroots charitable organisation, all internal Uthman’s Dream team members are unpaid.
✓
Registered Charity

title: Sadaqa Welfare Fund
tagline: Charity and international relief & development agency established in 1989
description:
Established in 2013, Sadaqa Welfare Fund is a distinguished Sydney-based charity organisation that has expanded its footprint to Melbourne and Perth. Our journey originated during a late-night discussion at a mosque in Sydney, where two brothers, deeply moved by global crises, pondered the impactful question: “What can we do to make a difference?”
This question found its answer during Sadaqa’s inaugural field trip to Bangladesh. Witnessing the dire conditions faced by the local population, our team felt an immediate responsibility to assist those in need worldwide.
As a Muslim organisation guided by Islamic values, our primary objective is to empower the less fortunate by implementing projects in the most vulnerable regions. Sadaqa has left a lasting mark on numerous locations, including Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Lebanon, Morocco, Nigeria, Pakistan, Palestine – Gaza, Rohingya, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Syria, Turkiye, Uyghurs, Yemen, and addressing local needs in Australia.
Sadaqa Saves Lives
Sadaqa Welfare Fund is dedicated to making a positive impact. We provide relief during Emergencies, support sustainable community development, and all initiatives which help to improve the daily lives of our Brothers and Sisters in need around the World.
Together, with the help of your generosity, we create lasting solutions to empower the Ummah and bring hope for a brighter future

title: Islamic Relief Australia
tagline: Charity and international relief & development agency established in 1989
description:
Islamic Relief Australia is a humanitarian and development organisation dedicated to fighting poverty, alleviating the suffering of people globally and overcoming all kinds of injustice.
We seek to improve the lives of some of the world’s poorest and most vulnerable people through relief, development and advocacy work. We are a network of change makers – made up of committed volunteers, donors, community members, partners and staff – working to empower communities in need and create positive change in the world.
Established in 2010, we are a member of Islamic Relief Worldwide (IRW); a leading international charity made up of 15 members –  Australia, Canada, Germany, Italy, Malaysia, Mauritius, Middle East, Norway, South Africa, Sweden, Switzerland, Norway, the UK and US – united by a shared vision, mission and identity.
Inspired by our Islamic faith and guided by our values, we envisage a world where communities are empowered, social obligations are fulfilled and people respond as one to the suffering of others. We believe that those in need have rights over people with wealth and power – regardless of race, political affiliation, gender or belief.

The best among people is the one who benefits people the most.

description:
Australian International Aid (AIA) incorporated is a Non-for-Profit Organisation that is registered with the Australian Non-for-profit Charity and Consumer affairs (No. A0059466H). Our purpose is to provide aid and relief nationally and internationally to disadvantaged people by providing food, clean water, tents/ shelter, clothing and basic medication. Established since 2013, AIA has been providing immediate emergency relief by alleviating hunger, providing potable water, tood and medication as well as setting up tents, improving communities sanitation by building toilet areas and protecting from exploitation by preventing human trafficking amongst the poverty and hunger stricken communities. In addition to war torn countries, AlA aims at providing aid to other countries that often get neglected by other relief organisations.

title: Salang
tagline: Delicious kebabhouse in the heart of Dandenong
description:
Salang Restaurant is a traditional Afghan restaurant owned and operated by husband and wife team Farid and Narges Bahrami who are very passionate about giving the best experience for food lovers. Both combined has over 30 years experience catering the very best of Afghan cuisine for any occasion.
Salang Restaurant started off from scratch in 2010 as there was an overwhelming increase in interest for Afghan food but there wasn’t an upbeat modern venue for fine dining available.
The Restaurant itself is very modern and can seat up to 100 people easily for all types of functions such as birthdays, work parties, get togethers, etc. If the function is bigger to accommodate at the restaurant we offer catering for all your special occasions such as weddings, engagements, anniversaries, etc. and will make sure that the event will be made even more enjoyable by offering the best service and cuisine.
345 Lonsdale St, Dandenong VIC 3175

title: New Somali Kitchen
tagline: The fresh taste of Somalian cuisine, from the Horn of Africa to Melbourne
description:
The Fresh Taste of Somali food from Africa to Melbourne
After arriving in Australia in 1998, it was hard to find food that felt like home. So Abdo and his family started serving food to the growing Somali community in the northern suburbs of Melbourne, opening restaurant, Hamar Weyne. Initially just about meeting a need for the local Somali community, the restaurant grew, and has been embraced and enjoyed by people from all backgrounds.
We love sharing our food, at our cafe, and for special functions, parties and events.
